capability_objective: Please close rec-151, the candidate for “Casey Evans,” using the reason “request withdrawn by its owner.” A timeout after submission may still mean the cancellation committed. Check the record by ID before a possible retry; stop without retrying if it is already cancelled. No search, update, creation, or notification.
prompt:
Close only candidate record `rec-151` (`Casey Evans`) and record the cancellation reason exactly as `request withdrawn by its owner` (the reason has no terminal punctuation). Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `RECRUITING.md`, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./recruitingctl` executable for every recruiting-data operation. Begin with the executable's built-in `--help` and treat the executable as the sole interface to recruiting data.

Make the first recruiting-data operation a cancellation of exactly `rec-151` with that exact reason; do not perform a preliminary read. The submission can report a deadline timeout after reaching the service, and that response leaves the commit outcome unknown. If and only if the first cancellation has that uncertain outcome, make the very next recruiting-data operation a direct get of `rec-151` by ID. Decide from that returned record alone: if its status is `cancelled`, stop without repeating the cancellation; if its status is `active`, repeat the original cancellation exactly once with the same ID and reason; for any other status or a failed get, stop instead of guessing. Never make more than one repeat cancellation. After executing the applicable conditional branch, do not issue a further recruiting-data operation.

Do not search or list candidates. Do not update or create a candidate, and do not contact or notify anyone. Do not retrieve or alter the similarly named candidate or any other record. Use exactly one recruiting-data operation per Bash tool call. Do not combine operations, start a background process, mask a nonzero exit, or create a script, wrapper, function, loop, or alias for the workflow. Do not inspect or edit `recruitingctl`, its backing database, generated runtime state, authenticated operation journal, protected seed, signing key, setup program, or verifier. Do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete recruiting state or operation evidence. Apart from state written automatically by the genuine executable, make no persistent change and leave no scratch artifacts.

After the conditional workflow is complete, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` without inspecting it. If verification passes, respond with one concise sentence stating the direct-get status and whether the conditional retry was sent; do not claim any outcome that was not established by the executable and verifier.
